rows.query: after adjustment for inflation, federal s&e obligations to academic institutions increased by 4% between fy 2013 and fy 2014.
rows.code:
col = "all federal obligations"
a_val = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('constant fy 2009 $millions > 2013'), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
b_val = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('constant fy 2009 $millions > 2014'), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = ((b_val - a_val) / a_val) * 100
print(result)

rows.query: approximately three-quarters of total completed cases involving with multiple charges resulted in a guilty outcome.
rows.code:
col = "multiple-charge cases > percent"
vals = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')
result = vals.iloc[0]
print(result)

rows.query: r&d plant funding increased substantially by 27%.
rows.code:
col = "current $millions > 2017 projected"
proj_2017 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('r&d plant'), col], errors='coerce').sum()
col = "current $millions > 2016 preliminary"
prelim_2016 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('r&d plant'), col], errors='coerce').sum()
increase = (proj_2017 - prelim_2016) / prelim_2016 * 100
result = increase
print(result)

rows.query: 35 states reported expenditures for healthrelated r&d in fy 2016, yet 83% of all state government agency expenditures on health-related r&d was reported by agencies in five states: new york , texas , california , florida , and pennsylvania.
rows.code:
col = "health"
cali = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('california', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
tx = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('texas', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
ny = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('new york', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
fl = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('florida', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
pa = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('pennsylvania', case=False, na=False), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
total = df[col].sum()
result = cali + tx + ny + fl + pa
print(result / total * 100)

rows.query: 52% of family class immigrants came from south asia, east asia and western developed countries.
rows.code:
family_class_col = "family class"
south_asia = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('percent > source region > southern asia'), family_class_col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
east_asia = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('percent > source region > east asia'), family_class_col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
western_developed = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('percent > source region > western developed countries'), family_class_col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = south_asia + east_asia + western_developed
print(result)

rows.query: after adjustment for inflation, federal s&e obligations to academic institutions declined by 7% from fy 2012 to fy 2013.
rows.code:
col = "all federal obligations"
fy_2012 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('current \$millions > 2012'), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
fy_2013 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('current \$millions > 2013'), col], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
decline = ((fy_2012 - fy_2013) / fy_2012) * 100
result = abs(decline - 7)
print(result)

rows.query: according to the 2014/2015 integrated criminal court survey, one in five completed court cases involved a female accused.
rows.code:
total = pd.to_numeric(df['total > number'], errors='coerce').sum()
females = pd.to_numeric(df['youth court > females > number'], errors='coerce').sum() + pd.to_numeric(df['adult court > females > number'], errors='coerce').sum()
result = females / total * 5
print(result)

rows.query: the unemployment rate of arab women was 10.7 percentage points higher than that of women who did not belong to a visible minority group.

non_visible_unemployment = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['visible minority'].str.contains('not a visible minority', case=False, na=False), 'total'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = arab_unemployment - non_visible_unemployment
print(result)

rows.query: 30% of homicides perpetrated by a female accused involved a victim who was either her spouse or dating partner.

rows.query: out-of-the-labour-force inuit were more than twice as likely as employed inuit to gather for monetary reasons.
rows.code:

employed = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('employed'), 'col[10]'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = out_of_labour_force / employed > 2
print(result)

rows.query: the number of postdocs doing research in science fields in 2019 is similar to that in 2012, although the percentage has declined about 12 points.
rows.code:
total_2012 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['2012 > total'].notna(), '2012 > total'], errors='coerce').sum()
total_2019 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['2019 > total'].notna(), '2019 > total'], errors='coerce').sum()
percentage_change = (total_2019 - total_2012) / total_2012 * 100
result = abs(percentage_change).round()
print(result)

rows.query: within lone-parent households, employed first nations women were less likely to be food secure than men.

female_food_secure = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('lone-parent household', case=False, na=False) & df['col_header'].str.contains('women', case=False, na=False), 'percent > food secure household'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = male_food_secure - female_food_secure
print(result)

rows.query: older women experienced lower earnings growth than their younger counterparts.
rows.code:
men_se = pd.to_numeric(df['men > standard error'], errors='coerce')
women_se = pd.to_numeric(df['women > standard error'], errors='coerce')
men_coef = pd.to_numeric(df['men > coefficient'], errors='coerce')
women_coef = pd.to_numeric(df['women > coefficient'], errors='coerce')
older_women = women_coef[df.index >= 1].max()
younger_women = women_coef[df.index < 1].max()
older_men = men_coef[df.index >= 1].max()
younger_men = men_coef[df.index < 1].max()
result = (older_women - younger_women) - (older_men - younger_men)
print(result)

rows.query: the three largest cmas, toronto which accounted for 48% of all robberies reported by police in 2015, had smaller changes.
rows.code:
toronto_robbery_rate = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col'].str.contains('toronto'), 'robbery > rate'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
other_cmas_robbery_rate = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['col'].str.contains('homicide|sexual assault|breaking and entering|motor vehicle theft'), 'robbery > rate']).sum()
total_robbery_rate = toronto_robbery_rate + other_cmas_robbery_rate
percent_toronto = (toronto_robbery_rate / total_robbery_rate) * 100
result = percent_toronto >= 48
print(result)

rows.query: in 2016/2017, there were 5,937 youth that began a period of supervision in correctional services in the eight reporting jurisdictions, a 44% decrease from 2012/2013.
rows.code:

percent_change_2012_2013 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'] == '(total correctional services)', 'total-all jurisdictions > percent change in the number from 2012/2013'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
result = total_2016_2017 / (1 + percent_change_2012_2013 / 100)
print(result)

rows.query: in 2018/2019, the rate of adults on probation decreased by 6% compared to 2017/2018.
rows.code:
probation_rate_2018 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('probation', case=False, na=False), 'probate > rate'], errors='coerce').iloc[0]
probation_rate_2017 = pd.to_numeric(df.loc[df['row_header'].str.contains('probation', case=False, na=False), 'probate > rate'].shift(1), errors='coerce').iloc[1]
percent_change = (probation_rate_2018 - probation_rate_2017) / probation_rate_2017 * 100
result = percent_change.round().iloc[0]
print(result)
